content:
# Leviticus Chapter 15 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 67r–67v | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/LEV_015_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_015_page_002.jpg` | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 101–102

---

**1** And the LORD spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,

**2** "Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When any man has a discharge from his body, his discharge is unclean.

**3** And this shall be his uncleanness in his discharge: whether his body flows with his discharge or his body is stopped from his discharge, it is his uncleanness.

**4** Every bed on which the one who has the discharge lies shall be unclean, and everything on which he sits shall be unclean.

**5** And anyone who touches his bed shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**6** And whoever sits on anything on which the one who has the discharge has sat shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**7** And whoever touches the body of the one who has the discharge shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**8** And if the one who has the discharge spits on one who is clean, then he shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**9** And any saddle on which the one who has the discharge rides shall be unclean.

**10** And whoever touches anything that was under him shall be unclean until the evening. And whoever carries those things shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**11** And anyone whom the one who has the discharge touches without having rinsed his hands in water shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**12** And an earthen vessel that the one who has the discharge touches shall be broken, and every vessel of wood shall be rinsed with water.

**13** "And when the one who has the discharge is cleansed of his discharge, he shall count for himself seven days for his cleansing, and he shall wash his clothes, and he shall bathe his body in fresh water, and he shall be clean.

**14** And on the eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two pigeons and come before the LORD to the entrance of the tent of meeting and give them to the priest.

**15** And the priest shall offer them, one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering. And the priest shall make atonement for him before the LORD for his discharge.

**16** "If a man has an emission of seed, he shall bathe his whole body in water and be unclean until the evening.

**17** And every garment and every skin on which there is an emission of seed shall be washed with water and be unclean until the evening.

**18** And if a man lies with a woman and there is an emission of seed, both of them shall bathe themselves in water and be unclean until the evening.

**19** "And if a woman has a discharge, and her discharge in her body is blood, she shall be in her menstrual impurity [niddah] for seven days. And whoever touches her shall be unclean until the evening.

**20** And everything on which she lies during her menstrual impurity shall be unclean, and everything on which she sits shall be unclean.

**21** And whoever touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**22** And whoever touches anything on which she sits shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**23** Whether it is the bed or anything on which she sits, when he touches it he shall be unclean until the evening.

**24** And if any man lies with her and her menstrual impurity comes upon him, he shall be unclean seven days, and every bed on which he lies shall be unclean.

**25** "If a woman has a discharge of blood for many days, not at the time of her menstrual impurity, or if she has a discharge beyond the time of her menstrual impurity, all the days of the discharge of her uncleanness shall be as in the days of her menstrual impurity: she is unclean.

**26** Every bed on which she lies all the days of her discharge shall be to her as the bed of her menstrual impurity, and everything on which she sits shall be unclean, as in the uncleanness of her menstrual impurity.

**27** And whoever touches these things shall be unclean, and shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water and be unclean until the evening.

**28** But if she is cleansed from her discharge, she shall count for herself seven days, and after that she shall be clean.

**29** And on the eighth day she shall take for herself two turtledoves or two pigeons and bring them to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting.

**30** And the priest shall offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering. And the priest shall make atonement for her before the LORD for her unclean discharge.

**31** "Thus you shall separate the sons of Israel from their uncleanness, lest they die in their uncleanness by defiling my tabernacle that is in their midst.

**32** This is the law for the one who has a discharge and for him who has an emission of seed, becoming unclean by it,

**33** and for her who is sick in her menstrual impurity, and for anyone, male or female, who has a discharge, and for the man who lies with a woman who is unclean."

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations

**Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 67r–67v:**
Leviticus 15 occupies two folios in the Leningrad Codex and is well preserved with no major lacunae. The chapter exhibits a tightly parallel structure characteristic of priestly legislation. Two male conditions (abnormal discharge, vv. 1–15; normal seminal emission, vv. 16–18) are paired with two female conditions (normal menstruation/niddah, vv. 19–24; abnormal discharge/zavah, vv. 25–30), followed by a legislative summary (vv. 31–33). The Hebrew employs highly repetitive formulaic language — particularly the phrase "he/she shall wash his/her clothes and bathe himself/herself in water and be unclean until the evening" — which is preserved in full in the translation rather than condensed.

The key term zav (one who has a discharge, masc.) appears consistently in the MT with the article (ha-zav) in the generalizing sense throughout vv. 2–13. The verbal root zub ("to flow") underlies zav, zov (the discharge itself), and zavah (the female counterpart). This root-linkage is noted in the term renderings below.

The term niddah (v. 19) carries the sense of removal/separation (root ndh, "to thrust away, to put at a distance") as well as the menstrual condition itself. It is preserved with a bracketed gloss on first occurrence.

**Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 101–102:**
The LXX of Leviticus 15 in Vaticanus follows the MT structure closely. The Greek translator renders zav consistently as ῥύσις (rhysis, "flow/flux") and ha-zav as ὁ ῥέων ("the one who flows"). The female zavah becomes ἡ ῥέουσα. The word niddah is rendered ἀφεδρεία (aphedreia, "menstrual separation"), consistently used throughout the LXX for this technical term. No significant transpositions of verses are attested in Vaticanus for this chapter.

---

### Key Translation Decisions

**1. "Discharge from his body" (v. 2) — zov mibsaro:**
The Hebrew zov (from zub, "to flow") denotes a pathological discharge from the genitalia, distinct from normal seminal emission. The phrase mibsaro ("from his body/flesh") is a priestly euphemism for the genitals, paralleling similar usage in Leviticus 6:3; 13:18; and Ezekiel 16:26. "From his body" is retained as a formal rendering that preserves the Hebrew euphemism rather than specifying the anatomical referent directly, consistent with the dignified register of priestly law.

**2. "Whether his body flows… or his body is stopped" (v. 3) — raz besaro / he'tim besaro:**
Verse 3 is syntactically difficult. The two alternatives describe the condition as unclean whether the discharge is active (raz, "flows") or intermittent/obstructed (he'tim, from atam, "to stop up, to seal"). Some interpreters read he'tim as describing mucous obstruction or intermission. The translation renders both alternatives with the same subject ("his body") to preserve the Hebrew chiastic structure.

**3. "Emission of seed" (vv. 16–18) — shkhvat-zera:**
The compound shkhvat-zera (literally "lying of seed," i.e., semen) refers to seminal ejaculation as distinct from the pathological zov. "Emission of seed" is the most precise formal equivalent, preferred over the vague "bodily discharge" (some modern translations) or the overly clinical "seminal emission." The term is rendered consistently in vv. 16–18.

**4. "Menstrual impurity" for niddah (vv. 19–26):**
Niddah is rendered "menstrual impurity" rather than simply "menstruation" to capture both the physical condition and its ritual-legal status. The root ndh connotes separation and exclusion, not merely the physiological event. The gloss [niddah] is supplied at first occurrence (v. 19) and not repeated thereafter. The LXX ἀφεδρεία ("monthly separation") supports this dual sense.

**5. "Discharge of blood for many days" (v. 25) — dam rav:**
The phrase dam rav (literally "much/abundant blood") denotes an abnormal hemorrhagic discharge beyond the normal menstrual period — the condition of the zavah. This is the condition described in the Gospel narrative (Matt 9:20; Mark 5:25; Luke 8:43) of the woman with a "flow of blood for twelve years." "Discharge of blood for many days" renders the idiom fully. The qualifier "not at the time of her menstrual impurity, or if she has a discharge beyond the time of her menstrual impurity" renders the double qualification in the Hebrew, which distinguishes the zavah from the niddah.

**6. "Fresh water" (v. 13) — mayim chayyim:**
As in Leviticus 14:5–6, mayim chayyim ("living water") is rendered "fresh water," denoting running or spring water required for cleansing rituals. See the note on this term in LEV_014.

**7. "You shall separate" (v. 31) — vehizartem:**
The Hiphil of nzr ("to separate, to set apart") is rendered "you shall separate." This is a Hiphil causative: Moses and Aaron are commanded to cause the people to be set apart from their uncleanness. Some translations render "you shall warn" (reading the root as related to a different verb), but vehizartem is better understood as a causative of separation in this priestly context.

**8. "My tabernacle" (v. 31) — mishkani:**
The first-person possessive "my tabernacle" (mishkani) is significant: the LORD speaks of the tabernacle as his own dwelling. "Tabernacle" is the standard rendering of mishkan ("dwelling place") throughout Leviticus, distinguished from ohel moed ("tent of meeting"). Both terms refer to the same structure from different angles — mishkan emphasizing its character as divine dwelling, ohel moed emphasizing its function as the place where God meets his people.

**9. "She who is sick in her menstrual impurity" (v. 33) — davah beniddatah:**
The participle davah (from dvh, "to be faint/sick") combined with beniddatah ("in her menstrual impurity") is an expression for the menstruating woman. "Sick in her menstrual impurity" preserves both the verbal and nominal components of the phrase. The LXX renders ἡ καθημένη ἐν τῇ ἀφεδρείᾳ ("she who sits in her menstrual separation"), capturing the social dimension of separation.

**10. Summary verse (v. 33):**
The closing verse is an extended participial phrase that recapitulates all the conditions treated in the chapter. It functions as a legal colophon. The translation preserves its list character by rendering each condition in parallel. The final phrase "the man who lies with a woman who is unclean" closes the chapter by returning to the sexual contact legislation of vv. 18 and 24, framing the chapter with the theme of contact-transmitted impurity.

---

### LXX Vaticanus Variants

| Verse | MT (Hebrew) | LXX (Vaticanus) | Note |
|-------|-------------|-----------------|------|
| 2 | zov mibsaro ("discharge from his body") | ῥύσις ἐκ τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ | Close formal match; LXX uses ῥύσις ("flow/flux") for zov throughout |
| 3 | raz besaro / he'tim besaro ("flows / stopped") | ῥέοι τὸ σῶμα αὐτοῦ / συγκεκλεισμένη ἡ ῥύσις | LXX: "his body flows" / "the flux is shut up" — good functional match |
| 9 | merkav ("saddle") | ἐπίβλημα ("covering/overlay") | LXX diverges slightly; merkav = riding-gear, but LXX reads more generally |
| 13 | mayim chayyim ("fresh/living water") | ὕδωρ ζῶν | Literal Greek rendering retained |
| 19 | niddah ("menstrual impurity") | ἀφεδρεία | Standard LXX equivalent for niddah; conveys separation |
| 24 | u-tefas niddatah alav ("and her menstrual impurity comes upon him") | καὶ γένηται ἡ ἀκαθαρσία αὐτῆς ἐπ᾽ αὐτόν | LXX: "her uncleanness comes upon him" — slight softening of the MT idiom |
| 25 | dam rav ("much blood") | ῥύσει αἵματος πολλαῖς ἡμέραις | LXX expands: "a flow of blood for many days" — good semantic match |
| 27 | ve-tameh ("and shall be unclean") | καὶ ἀκάθαρτος ἔσται | Standard LXX equivalence |
| 31 | mishkani ("my tabernacle") | σκηνῆς μου | LXX: "my tent/tabernacle" — exact equivalence |
| 31 | vehizartem ("you shall separate") | καὶ ἐκκλινεῖτε ("and you shall turn away") | LXX interprets differently: "you shall cause them to turn away" rather than causative separation |
| 33 | davah beniddatah ("sick in her menstrual impurity") | ἡ καθημένη ἐν τῇ ἀφεδρείᾳ | LXX: "she who sits in her separation" — emphasizes social isolation over physical condition |

---

### Consistent Term Renderings

| Hebrew Term | LOGOS Rendering | Notes |
|-------------|-----------------|-------|
| zav | the one who has the discharge | Rendered with article as a legal category; not transliterated in text body |
| zov | discharge | Verbal noun from zub ("to flow"); rendered uniformly |
| shkhvat-zera | emission of seed | Formal compound for seminal emission; distinguished from zov |
| niddah | menstrual impurity | Rendered "menstrual impurity"; gloss [niddah] on first occurrence |
| zavah | (she who has an abnormal discharge) | The legal category is described rather than transliterated; see note on v. 25 |
| dam rav | discharge of blood for many days | Full contextual rendering of "much blood" |
| mayim chayyim | fresh water | As established in Lev 14; noted as "living water" in notes |
| mishkan | tabernacle | Consistent with Lev 8–14 |
| ohel moed | tent of meeting | Consistent with Lev 1–14 |
| YHWH | the LORD | Small capitals as conventional |
| kohen | priest | Standard rendering |
| chattat | sin offering | Consistent with Lev 1–14 |
| olah | burnt offering | Consistent with Lev 1–14 |
| tameh | unclean | Standard Levitical rendering |
| tahor | clean | Standard Levitical rendering |
| kipper | make atonement | Pi'el rendered with full phrase |
| bney yisrael | sons of Israel | Formal rendering throughout |

---

— LOGOS
